checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant on STATE-DA-FAILED: Blocks with unavailable data MAY be queued for later reprocessing but

reachability.notes: Race condition: if queued blocks are later accepted without re-verification.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: Blocks with unavailable data MAY be queued for later reprocessing but MUST NOT be accepted into the store without confirmed data availability.
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'STATE-DA-FAILED'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions
   b. Boundary value inputs to state-changing operations
   c. Concurrent or re-entrant state modifications
4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: If queued blocks bypass DA verification on reprocessing, invalid data could be accepted.
executable_checks: Create state transition sequence targeting STATE-DA-FAILED invariant violation
Test boundary values for state transitions affecting STATE-DA-FAILED
Verify invariant holds under concurrent modifications
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W0-FULUFC-INV-004. Type: Invariant. Primary element: STATE-DA-FAILED.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant on ACTION-DETERMINE-SAMPLE-SET: The number of groups sampled per slot must be at least max(SAMPLES_PER

reachability.notes: Under-sampling would reduce data availability verification confidence.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: The number of groups sampled per slot must be at least max(SAMPLES_PER_SLOT, custody_group_count), ensuring sufficient sampling coverage.
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'ACTION-DETERMINE-SAMPLE-SET'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions
   b. Boundary value inputs to state-changing operations
   c. Concurrent or re-entrant state modifications
4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Insufficient sampling weakens DA guarantees, potentially allowing data withholding attacks to succeed against >5% of the network.
executable_checks: Create state transition sequence targeting ACTION-DETERMINE-SAMPLE-SET invariant violation
Test boundary values for state transitions affecting ACTION-DETERMINE-SAMPLE-SET
Verify invariant holds under concurrent modifications
checklist_item.notes: Source property: PROP-W1-DASCORE-INV-004. Type: Invariant. Primary element: ACTION-DETERMINE-SAMPLE-SET.

3. Verify that STATE-PEER-INCOMPATIBLE correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.
4. Check internal invariants are maintained even if boundary validation is partially bypassed.

2. Property context: Before accepting a data_column_sidecar, the node MUST verify verify_data_column_sidecar_kzg_proofs: batch cell KZG proof verification of column data against commitments (rule 11). Invalid KZG proofs M
3. Verify that STATE-DATA-COLUMN-SIDECAR-REJECTED correctly validates/processes data that has crossed the trust boundary.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition on ACTION-CALC-RESERVE-EXCESS: The 'max' and 'target' values used in the reserve mode formula must be

reachability.notes: Using wrong max/target parameters would produce incorrect excess calculations.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Pre-condition: The 'max' and 'target' values used in the reserve mode formula must be MAX_BLOB_GAS_PER_BLOCK and TARGET_BLOB_GAS_PER_BLOCK from the EIP-7840 blobSchedule for the current fork.
2. Enumerate inputs that should be rejected by this pre-condition.
3. Attempt bypass with:
   a. Invalid/malformed inputs that may slip through validation
   b. Edge case values (zero, max, negative, empty)
   c. Type confusion or encoding tricks
4. Verify correct rejection of all invalid inputs.
5. Check if bypassing leads to exploitable state.
6. Severity justification: Wrong parameters in the formula would cause all blocks to have incorrect excess_blob_gas, splitting the network.
executable_checks: Attempt precondition bypass on ACTION-CALC-RESERVE-EXCESS with crafted invalid inputs
Test edge-case values (zero, max, overflow) against precondition
Verify rejection behavior is consistent across all code paths

checklist_item.title: Falsify Pre-condition on ACTION-STORE-SIDECAR: A DataColumnSidecar MUST only be stored if it belongs to a custody gro

reachability.notes: A malicious peer could flood sidecars for non-custody groups to exhaust storage.

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Pre-condition: A DataColumnSidecar MUST only be stored if it belongs to a custody group that this validator is responsible for. Sidecars for non-custody groups SHOULD be rejected at the storage layer.
2. Enumerate inputs that should be rejected by this pre-condition.
3. Attempt bypass with:
   a. Invalid/malformed inputs that may slip through validation
   b. Edge case values (zero, max, negative, empty)
   c. Type confusion or encoding tricks
4. Verify correct rejection of all invalid inputs.
5. Check if bypassing leads to exploitable state.
6. Severity justification: Storage exhaustion via non-custody sidecars affects individual nodes, not network-wide.

Verify rejection behavior is consistent across all code paths

checklist_item.detection_procedure:
1. Invariant: Nodes that have not implemented EIP-7642 (optional networking upgrade) MUST remain able to participate in consensus and not be partitioned from the network.
2. Enumerate all state transitions affecting 'STATE-POST-PECTRA'.
3. Design test sequences to violate the invariant through:
   a. Rapid successive state transitions
   b. Boundary value inputs to state-changing operations
   c. Concurrent or re-entrant state modifications
4. Verify the invariant holds after each transition.
5. Check edge cases: empty state, maximum state, overflow.
6. Severity justification: Network partition of nodes without optional EIP could affect >5% of the network.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for Column Sidecar KZG Proof Verification in Fork Choice

reachability.notes: Attacker can send column sidecars with invalid KZG proofs via P2P gossip.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft a DataColumnSidecar with valid structural format but invalid KZG cell proofs. 2. Send the sidecar via P2P gossip to target nodes. 3. Verify the node rejects the sidecar and penalizes the sending peer. 4. Test with proofs that are valid points on BLS12-381 but don't correspond to the commitment. 5. Test with the identity element as a proof. 6. Verify that the fork choice rule does not consider blocks with unverified column data as having satisfied data availability.
executable_checks: Craft DataColumnSidecar with forged KZG proofs
Send via gossip and verify rejection
Test that fork choice excludes blocks lacking verified DA
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-PRECOND-007. Boundary edge EDGE-P2P-SIDECARS-TO-DA. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. Covers KZG proof verification at the fork choice DA boundary. Bypassing this allows fake data availability.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for Req/Resp Column Validation Parity with Gossip

reachability.notes: Columns received via Req/Resp must undergo identical validation as gossip-received columns.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Send an invalid DataColumnSidecar via gossip and verify it is rejected. 2. Send the same invalid sidecar via Req/Resp DataColumnSidecarsByRoot. 3. Verify it is also rejected with the same validation result. 4. Test all validation steps: format, inclusion proof, KZG proofs. 5. Verify that any validation step skipped in the Req/Resp path but present in gossip represents a bypass vulnerability.
executable_checks: Send identical invalid sidecar via both gossip and Req/Resp
Compare validation outcomes for both paths
Enumerate validation steps in both code paths and diff
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-DASCORE-PRECOND-007. Boundary edge EDGE-REQRESP-COLUMN-REQUEST. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. Validation asymmetry between gossip and Req/Resp is a common class of bugs.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Invariant: Served Sidecars Must Be Within Advertised Range and Valid

reachability.notes: Node must only serve sidecars within data_column_serve_range and that have passed gossip validation.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Request DataColumnSidecars via DataColumnSidecarsByRange for a range within data_column_serve_range. 2. Verify all returned sidecars are valid (pass full validation). 3. Request sidecars outside the serve range and verify ResourceUnavailable error (code 3). 4. Verify response ordering: ascending by (slot, column_index). 5. Verify response count does not exceed MAX_REQUEST_DATA_COLUMN_SIDECARS.
executable_checks: Request sidecars within and outside serve range
Validate all served sidecars pass full verification
Verify response ordering and count limits
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-FULU-REQRESP-INV-01. Internal property (Invariant), Formal Verification mindset. Ensures Req/Resp serving correctness.

Test gossip messages on topics with wrong fork digest prefix. 4. Verify they are rejected. 5. Test during the fork transition: verify peers with next_fork_digest (nfd) set correctly are not prematurely disconnected.
executable_checks: Connect with wrong fork digest ENR post-Fulu
Verify disconnection after grace period
Test gossip topic fork digest filtering
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-FORK-DIGEST-POST-002. Internal property (Post-condition), Formal Verification mindset. Fork digest enforcement prevents cross-fork communication.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Post-condition: All Cell Proofs Verified Before Mempool Admission

reachability.notes: Mempool admission must guarantee all proofs verified.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Trace the blob transaction validation pipeline in the codebase. 2. Verify cell proof batch verification occurs BEFORE mempool insertion. 3. Test with a blob transaction where one cell proof is invalid among otherwise valid proofs. 4. Verify the entire transaction is rejected (not partially accepted). 5. Verify the rejection prevents P2P propagation of the invalid transaction.

Submit blob TX with one invalid cell proof
Verify complete rejection and no P2P propagation

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for Cell Index Bounds in KZG Batch Verification

reachability.notes: Attacker sends cell_index >= CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB to trigger out-of-bounds access.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Send a P2P message containing cell data with cell_index = CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB (one past the valid range). 2. Verify the node rejects the input without crashing. 3. Test with cell_index = CELLS_PER_EXT_BLOB - 1 (valid, should be accepted). 4. Test with cell_index = MAX_UINT64. 5. Verify bounds check occurs before root-of-unity table lookup.

Verify rejection without crash
Test with maximum uint64 cell index
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-PCS-VERIFY-PRECOND-003. Boundary edge EDGE-CELL-VERIFY-P2P. Trust level: UNTRUSTED. Out-of-bounds access in root-of-unity tables could crash nodes.

checklist_item.title: Bypass CLZ input size validation with non-256-bit inputs
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Invoke CLZ opcode with inputs of varying sizes: 0 bytes, 1 byte, 31 bytes, 32 bytes (valid), 33 bytes, 64 bytes, 256 bytes. 2. Monitor EVM stack behavior for each input size. 3. Verify 32-byte inputs are processed correctly. 4. For non-32-byte inputs, verify behavior follows EVM stack semantics (stack underflow, padding behavior, or error). 5. Attempt to bypass validation by manipulating stack state before CLZ invocation. 6. Test edge cases: inputs with leading zeros, all-zero inputs, maximum values. 7. Document any acceptance of non-256-bit inputs or deviation from EVM semantics.
executable_checks: Invoke CLZ with 0-byte input - verify EVM stack underflow or error
Invoke CLZ with 16-byte input - verify EVM stack semantics followed
Invoke CLZ with 31-byte input - verify EVM stack semantics followed
Invoke CLZ with 32-byte input - verify correct processing
Invoke CLZ with 33-byte input - verify EVM stack semantics followed
Invoke CLZ with 64-byte input - verify EVM stack semantics followed
Test stack state manipulation before CLZ - verify no bypass of validation
Test malformed stack with CLZ invocation - verify graceful error handling
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W0-EIP7939-PRECOND-002 covering CLZ opcode input validation

checklist_item.title: Violate column request limit invariant by exceeding C_req=8
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft GetCells messages requesting varying column counts (1, 8, 9, 16, 128, 256). 2. Submit via P2P to ACTION-REQUEST-CELLS. 3. Verify requests with count > C_req=8 are rejected before processing. 4. Test boundary: exactly 8 columns should succeed, 9 should fail. 5. Monitor bandwidth usage for large requests. 6. Attempt to bypass limit through multiple concurrent requests. 7. Verify EDGE-PROVIDERS-OBSERVED and EDGE-CELLS-RECEIVED respect limit.

Send GetCells with 8 columns and verify acceptance at boundary
Send GetCells with 9 columns and verify rejection
Send GetCells with 128 columns and verify rejection
Send GetCells with 256 columns and verify rejection and no processing
Test concurrent GetCells requests totaling > 8 columns and verify handling
Verify ACTION-REQUEST-CELLS enforces C_req=8 limit before EDGE-PROVIDERS-OBSERVED
Monitor bandwidth: verify response size bounded even for max valid request
Verify EDGE-CELLS-RECEIVED only occurs for requests within limit
Attempt to bypass by fragmenting request into smaller sub-requests

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Trigger EDGE-BACKFILL-COMPLETE and EDGE-SUBSCRIBE-COMPLETE to reach STATE-DATA-COLUMNS-FULLY-OPERATIONAL. 2. Verify full data column serve capabilities are active. 3. Simulate various runtime events: chain reorgs, fork transitions, node restarts, storage errors, network interruptions. 4. After each event, verify that data column serve capabilities remain active. 5. Monitor for any state transitions out of STATE-DATA-COLUMNS-FULLY-OPERATIONAL.

Simulate network interruption and verify operational state is maintained upon reconnection
Monitor operational state throughout Fulu fork lifecycle and verify no unexpected transitions out
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W2-FULU-FORK-INV-02 covering node STATE-DATA-COLUMNS-FULLY-OPERATIONAL and edges EDGE-BACKFILL-COMPLETE, EDGE-SUBSCRIBE-COMPLETE

checklist_item.title: Falsify Proposer Lookahead Determinism Invariant Through Cross-Implementation Testing
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Create canonical beacon state S with known epoch, validator set, and randao seed. 2. Serialize state to ensure identical input across implementations. 3. Execute ACTION-COMPUTE-PROPOSER-INDICES on state S using multiple implementations. 4. Capture proposer_lookahead vector output from each. 5. Perform byte-by-byte comparison. 6. Test edge cases: maximum/minimal validator set, equal effective balances, boundary epoch values. 7. Verify STATE-LOOKAHEAD-UPDATED contains identical data across all implementations.
executable_checks: Generate fixed beacon state, compute proposer_lookahead in 5 different client implementations, verify all outputs byte-identical
Run same computation 1000 times on single implementation with identical state, verify output never changes
Test with validator set size = 1, verify all implementations produce same single-proposer lookahead
Test with validator set size = MAX_VALIDATORS, verify determinism holds at maximum scale
Create state with 100 validators having identical effective balance, verify shuffling determinism
Use randao seed = 0x0000...0000, verify all implementations handle edge case identically
Use randao seed = 0xFFFF...FFFF, verify deterministic behavior at maximum seed value
Construct state at epoch boundary (epoch = 0, epoch = FAR_FUTURE_EPOCH - 1), verify determinism
Introduce validator with slashed=true, verify lookahead deterministically excludes slashed validators
Cross-validate: Python spec output vs Rust/Go/Java/Nim implementations, assert zero divergence
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W3-FULU-BEACON-LOOKAHEAD-INV-004 covering ACTION-COMPUTE-PROPOSER-INDICES, STATE-LOOKAHEAD-UPDATED

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for OnChain_to_MODEXP: Reject MODEXP calls with length_of_BASE > 1024
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify MODEXP precompile entry point. 2. Craft transaction calling MODEXP with length_of_BASE = 1025. 3. Submit transaction on-chain. 4. Verify transaction reverts BEFORE any computation. 5. Test boundary: length_of_BASE = 1024 (accept) vs 1025 (reject).
executable_checks: Submit MODEXP call with length_of_BASE = 1025: verify immediate revert before computation
Submit MODEXP call with length_of_BASE = 1024: verify acceptance and correct execution
Submit MODEXP call with length_of_BASE = 2048: verify rejection
Resource monitoring: verify rejected calls consume minimal gas (only validation, no computation)
Fuzzing: send MODEXP calls with randomized BASE lengths 1000-2000, verify 1024 is exact boundary
Code review: verify BASE length check occurs first, before memory allocation or computation setup
DoS test: send 1000 requests with length_of_BASE = 10000, verify no resource exhaustion
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W5-EIP7823-VAL-PRECOND-001 covering trust boundary OnChain_to_MODEXP

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for P2P -> Sidecar Retrieval: Column Index Range Validation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify P2P sidecar request ingestion point. 2. Trace column indices from network message to storage lookup. 3. Locate boundary validation for [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS) range. 4. Attempt to bypass: send indices >= NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS, max uint64, etc. 5. Verify if out-of-bounds indices trigger array access violations, panics, or incorrect data returns.
executable_checks: Send P2P sidecar request with column_index = NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS and verify rejection
Send P2P sidecar request with column_index = max uint64 and verify rejection
Request column_index = NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS + 1000 and verify bounds check prevents out-of-bounds access
Flood peer with 10000 invalid column index requests and verify rate limiting or banning
Send column_index as non-integer type (if protocol allows) and verify parsing rejection
checklist_item.notes: Traced from PROP-W6-FULU-VAL-RETAIN-PRECOND-003 covering edge_p2p_sidecar_request

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-TX-TO-PRECOMPILE: secp256r1 point-at-infinity rejection

reachability.notes: Point-at-infinity attacks are a known ECDSA vulnerability.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct a transaction calling the secp256r1 precompile (0x100) with public key (qx=0, qy=0) — the point at infinity. 2. Submit via on-chain transaction. 3. Verify precompile returns empty output (0-length returndata). 4. Test with (0,0) in both compressed and uncompressed forms. 5. Cross-check behavior across all client implementations (Geth, Nethermind, Besu, Erigon, Reth) to confirm deterministic rejection.
executable_checks: Deploy contract calling 0x100 with (r, s, qx=0, qy=0) and assert returndata length == 0
Fuzz qx/qy with values (0,0), (p,0), (0,p) and verify consistent rejection
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7951-PRECOND-005. Boundary edge: EDGE-TX-TO-PRECOMPILE. The identity element must never be accepted as a valid public key.

checklist_item.title: Falsify CLZ opcode fork activation boundary behavior

reachability.notes: Fork boundary handling: transactions in the activation block must use the correct behavior.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Deploy a contract using opcode 0x1e in the block immediately BEFORE Fusaka activation. Verify it causes INVALID opcode exception. 2. Execute the same contract in the exact Fusaka activation block. Verify CLZ executes correctly. 3. Test the block BEFORE activation (off-by-one). 4. Cross-check all client implementations for identical activation block behavior.
executable_checks: Test: execute 0x1e opcode at block N-1 (pre-fork), assert INVALID
Test: execute 0x1e opcode at block N (fork block), assert CLZ result correct
Cross-client: compare gas consumption for CLZ across all clients
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7939-PRECOND-003. Related ambiguity: AMB-EIP7939-02. Pre-condition: fork activation boundary must be exact.

checklist_item.title: Falsify eth/69 handshake compatibility invariant maintenance throughout connection

reachability.notes: Ensures all connected peers remain compatible

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Complete an eth/69 handshake with a peer. 2. Verify networkid, genesis hash, and forkid are validated during handshake. 3. Attempt to violate: after handshake, simulate a fork update that makes the peer incompatible and verify the connection is dropped. 4. Test: peer sends Status with valid fields but then sends messages inconsistent with claimed fork version.
executable_checks: P2P test: complete handshake, then verify fork compatibility is rechecked at fork transitions
P2P test: peer with mismatched networkid during handshake, assert disconnect
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-EIP7642-HANDSHAKE-INV-01. Invariant: compatibility must hold throughout connection lifetime.

checklist_item.title: Falsify blob schedule synchronization across client implementations [CONDITIONAL SCOPE]

reachability.notes: No protocol-level enforcement of blob schedule updates. Relies on client teams shipping correct updates.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify all client implementations have identical BLOB_SCHEDULE configuration for each scheduled epoch. 2. Check default values in each client's config files. 3. Attempt to falsify: configure one client with a different blob schedule and verify it forks from the network. 4. NOTE: This is CONDITIONAL scope — requires further investigation whether protocol-level enforcement exists.
executable_checks: Cross-client: compare BLOB_SCHEDULE constants in all client source code
Test: client with wrong blob schedule, verify it rejects valid post-change blocks
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-BLOB-SCHEDULE-INV-004. CONDITIONAL SCOPE: no protocol-level enforcement. Related assumption: ASSUM-FULU-BEACON-04.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for blob transaction rejection on proof failure

reachability.notes: If failed verification does not properly reject, invalid blob transactions leak into the mempool and propagate.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Construct a type-3 blob transaction with valid structure but invalid cell KZG proofs. 2. Submit via P2P transaction gossip. 3. Verify the transaction is NOT added to the mempool. 4. Verify it is NOT re-gossiped to other peers. 5. Test: verify the rejection happens synchronously (no race between validation and mempool insertion).
executable_checks: P2P test: submit blob tx with invalid cell proofs, verify not in mempool
P2P test: verify invalid blob tx not re-gossiped
Test: verify no partial state change from rejected blob tx
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-BLOBTX-POSTCOND-002. Post-condition: failed proof verification MUST result in rejection.

checklist_item.title: Falsify history expiry activation sequence enforcement

reachability.notes: Premature mainnet activation before Pectra could break CL deposit processing.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify that history expiry configuration for mainnet requires Pectra fork to be active. 2. Attempt to enable history expiry before Pectra activation epoch. 3. Verify the client refuses or warns. 4. Test: check if there's a configuration flag that could bypass the Pectra prerequisite.
executable_checks: Config test: verify mainnet history expiry config requires PECTRA_ACTIVATION_EPOCH
Test: attempt activation before Pectra, assert rejection or warning
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-EIP7927-ACT-INV-001. Related ambiguity: AMB-EIP7927-01. Configuration-dependent but could break deposit processing.

checklist_item.title: Falsify deep copy isolation between parent and child beacon states
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Obtain a reference to the parent beacon state from the fork choice store. 2. Execute on_block which copies the parent state and applies state_transition. 3. After state_transition completes, compare the parent state in the store with a snapshot taken before the operation. 4. Specifically check mutable nested structures: (a) validator registry entries, (b) balances array, (c) slashings array, (d) participation flags, (e) proposer_lookahead array (new in Fulu). 5. Modify the child state and verify parent state is unaffected. 6. Language-specific: check for reference sharing in Python (id() comparison), Go (pointer comparison), Rust (Rc/Arc sharing).
executable_checks: Snapshot parent state hash before on_block, verify unchanged after
Mutate child state validator balance, verify parent validator balance unchanged
Check proposer_lookahead array in child vs parent for reference independence
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-FULUFC-INV-001. Invariant falsification. Covers nodes: STATE-PARENT-STATE-COPIED, ACTION-STATE-TRANSITION-EXEC. Shallow copy bug would corrupt the fork choice store across blocks, affecting all subsequent fork choice decisions.

checklist_item.title: Falsify independence and unpredictability of transaction role assignment PRNG
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Observe a node's role decisions (provider vs sampler) for a sequence of transaction announcements from the same peer. 2. Perform statistical tests: (a) chi-squared test for uniform distribution, (b) serial correlation test for sequential dependence, (c) runs test for randomness. 3. Attempt to predict future role decisions based on observed history (if PRNG is seeded with predictable inputs like peer_id or tx_hash). 4. Check if the announcing peer can influence the seed (e.g., by choosing specific tx hashes). 5. Verify the PRNG source is cryptographically secure (not math/rand or similar).
executable_checks: Collect 1000+ role decisions, run chi-squared test for uniformity
Attempt prediction of next role decision from observed sequence
Audit PRNG source: verify CSPRNG usage, not deterministic seed from peer-controlled data
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP8070-INV-001. Invariant falsification. Related ambiguity: AMB-EIP8070-01. If role is predictable, attacker can target providers to suppress data availability.

checklist_item.title: Falsify get_blob_parameters completeness for all post-Electra epochs
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Call get_blob_parameters for: (a) epoch == ELECTRA_FORK_EPOCH, (b) epoch == FULU_FORK_EPOCH - 1, (c) epoch == FULU_FORK_EPOCH, (d) epoch == FULU_FORK_EPOCH + 1, (e) epoch = far future (e.g., 2^32). 2. Verify all calls return valid BlobParameters (never None/null/error). 3. Verify Electra-era epochs return Electra defaults (target=3, max=6). 4. Verify Fulu-era epochs return correct updated parameters from BLOB_SCHEDULE. 5. Edge case: epoch between two BLOB_SCHEDULE entries — verify correct interpolation or lookup. 6. Edge case: empty BLOB_SCHEDULE — verify fallback to Electra defaults.
executable_checks: Call get_blob_parameters for every boundary epoch, verify non-null return
Verify Electra-era epochs return target=3, max=6
Verify Fulu-era epochs return updated parameters
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-BLOB-SCHEDULE-INV-001. Invariant falsification. Related ambiguity: AMB-FULU-BEACON-01. Missing blob params causes block validation failure or acceptance of invalid blocks.

checklist_item.title: Falsify EIP-7883 gas >= EIP-2565 gas for all MODEXP inputs
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Implement both the EIP-2565 and EIP-7883 gas formulas. 2. For each input combination (base_length, exponent_length, modulus_length) in ranges [0, 1024]: compute both gas costs. 3. Verify EIP-7883 >= EIP-2565 for ALL combinations. 4. Focus on edge cases: (a) very small inputs (all lengths = 1), (b) inputs near the new upper bounds (EIP-7823), (c) exponent with leading zeros, (d) exponent = 0, (e) modulus_length = 1 (minimum). 5. Use property-based testing (e.g., Hypothesis/QuickCheck) to search for counterexamples.
executable_checks: Compare EIP-7883 vs EIP-2565 gas for all (base_len, exp_len, mod_len) in [0, 32]
Property-based fuzz: random inputs in [0, 1024], assert EIP-7883 >= EIP-2565
Test specific edge: exponent = 0, modulus_length = 1
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W4-EIP7883-INV-002. Invariant falsification. Related ambiguity: AMB-EIP7883-01. Gas regression enables DoS via underpriced computation.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for transaction gas limit cap enforcement at mempool
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 16,777,217 (2^24 + 1) via P2P. 2. Verify it is rejected at mempool acceptance. 3. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 16,777,216 (2^24). Verify it is accepted. 4. Submit a transaction with gasLimit = 16,777,215 (2^24 - 1). Verify it is accepted. 5. Test all transaction types: legacy (type 0), EIP-2930 (type 1), EIP-1559 (type 2), EIP-4844 (type 3). 6. Verify the error message indicates gas limit cap violation.
executable_checks: Submit tx with gasLimit = 2^24 + 1, verify mempool rejection
Submit tx with gasLimit = 2^24, verify acceptance
Test all tx types (0, 1, 2, 3) with over-cap gasLimit
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7825-TX-PRECOND-001. Boundary property. Trust boundary: attacker submits transaction via P2P with controlled gasLimit. Over-cap transactions could cause resource exhaustion.

checklist_item.title: Falsify Fiat-Shamir challenge determinism and collision resistance in batch verification
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Compute the Fiat-Shamir challenge r for a batch verification with specific (commitments, cell_indices, cells). 2. Modify one cell slightly and recompute r. Verify r changes. 3. Swap two commitments and recompute r. Verify r changes. 4. Test with duplicate commitments: verify they are included in the hash (not deduplicated). 5. Verify the hash domain separation includes all batch parameters. 6. Attempt to find two different inputs that produce the same challenge (collision search). 7. Verify the implementation matches the spec exactly (byte ordering, encoding).
executable_checks: Compute r, modify one cell, verify r changes
Test with duplicate commitments, verify they affect r
Verify implementation matches spec byte-for-byte
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-PCS-VERIFY-INV-001. Invariant falsification. Related ambiguity: AMB-PCS-FULU-04. Fiat-Shamir weakness allows proof forgery, enabling invalid cells to pass batch verification across all nodes.

checklist_item.title: Falsify proposer_lookahead corruption detection and recovery behavior
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Verify that proposer_lookahead is recomputed deterministically from (effective_balances, RANDAO_seed) at each epoch boundary. 2. Corrupt the proposer_lookahead array in a test node's state (change one entry). 3. Verify the node detects the inconsistency on next block processing (proposer doesn't match expected). 4. Check what recovery action is taken: does the node resync, recompute, or continue with corrupt data? 5. If no recovery mechanism exists, confirm the node becomes unable to validate blocks correctly.
executable_checks: Assert proposer_lookahead is deterministic given same inputs
Assert corrupted lookahead causes block validation failures
Assert node behavior on corruption (resync vs crash vs silent failure)
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7917-INV-006. Related ambiguity AMB-EIP7917-02. No explicit recovery procedure exists for corrupted lookahead. Corrupted lookahead requires full resync, taking affected nodes offline.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-P2P-ETH69-BLOCKRANGEUPDATE: validate BlockRangeUpdate from untrusted peers
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Send a BlockRangeUpdate with earliestBlock > latestBlock. Verify peer rejects or ignores. 2. Send with latestBlock = MAX_UINT64. Verify no integer overflow in range computation. 3. Send with both = 0. Verify handled gracefully. 4. Send at rate exceeding once per epoch. Verify rate limiting. 5. Send valid range, then request blocks in that range from the peer - if peer can't serve, verify peer scoring penalty. 6. Test malformed RLP encoding of the message.
executable_checks: Assert earliestBlock > latestBlock is rejected
Assert MAX_UINT64 values don't cause overflow
Assert rate limiting prevents > 1 update per epoch per peer
Assert malformed RLP is handled without crash
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-EIP7642-HISTORY-PRECOND-01. Boundary property. Trust model edge EDGE-P2P-ETH69-BLOCKRANGEUPDATE: BlockRangeUpdate messages must be rate-limited, false ranges must be penalized.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-EL-PAYLOAD-VERIFICATION: EL verification required before block acceptance
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Send a block via P2P with a syntactically valid but semantically invalid execution payload (e.g., invalid state root, invalid transactions). 2. Verify the CL calls Engine API newPayload before accepting the block. 3. Mock the Engine API to return INVALID. Verify the CL rejects the block. 4. Test what happens if Engine API times out: does CL accept the block or wait? 5. Verify CL validates execution payload structure (blob_kzg_commitments count vs get_blob_parameters) independently of EL. 6. Test with Engine API returning SYNCING: verify block is not prematurely accepted.
executable_checks: Assert CL calls newPayload before block acceptance
Assert INVALID response causes block rejection
Assert SYNCING response does not cause premature acceptance
Assert timeout does not cause silent acceptance
Assert CL independently validates blob commitment count
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-EXEC-PAYLOAD-PRE-002. Boundary property. Trust model edge EDGE-EL-PAYLOAD-VERIFICATION: CL must validate independently before trusting EL result. Skipping EL verification would allow invalid state transitions.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-DISCOVERY-ENR-CUSTODY: prevent eclipse attacks on column peers
checklist_item.bug_class: Eclipse Attack / Sybil Attack on DA
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Set up a target node. Flood the discovery layer with Sybil nodes claiming custody of specific columns. 2. Monitor whether the target node's peer set becomes dominated by Sybil peers for those columns. 3. Verify the node enforces peer diversity (e.g., IP diversity, subnet diversity, ASN diversity). 4. Test if a node connects to multiple peers for the same custody group to avoid single-point-of-failure. 5. Verify ENR cgc (custody_group_count) is validated: reject peers with cgc < CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT. 6. Verify fork digest matching in ENR.
executable_checks: Assert peer diversity is enforced across custody groups
Assert ENR cgc >= CUSTODY_REQUIREMENT enforced
Assert ENR fork digest matching prevents cross-fork connections
Assert Sybil flood does not monopolize specific column peer sets
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-DAS-PRECOND-003. Boundary property. Trust model edge EDGE-DISCOVERY-ENR-CUSTODY: ENR fields must be validated, cgc must meet requirements. Eclipse attack could cause false data unavailability conclusions.

checklist_item.title: Verify DataColumnSidecar published to correct subnet via compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar
checklist_item.bug_class: Subnet Routing Error / Data Availability Degradation
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. For each column_index in [0, NUMBER_OF_COLUMNS), compute the subnet via compute_subnet_for_data_column_sidecar(column_index). 2. Verify the formula: column_index % DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT. 3. Publish a sidecar for column_index = 5. Verify it goes to subnet 5 % SUBNET_COUNT. 4. Monitor the actual pubsub topic used. Verify it matches data_column_sidecar_{subnet_id}. 5. Test all column indices to ensure coverage of all subnets.
executable_checks: Assert subnet = column_index % DATA_COLUMN_SIDECAR_SUBNET_COUNT for all columns
Assert published topic matches expected subnet
Assert all subnets receive approximately equal column distribution
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-VAL-SIDECAR-POSTCOND-002. Post-condition type. Wrong subnet mapping causes data column unavailability, affecting sampling for >5% of the network if the bug is in a popular client.

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-CELL-VERIFY-P2P: BLS subgroup checks on deserialization
checklist_item.bug_class: Cryptographic Soundness Break / Missing Subgroup Check
checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Craft a 48-byte value that represents a point on the BLS12-381 curve but NOT in the prime-order G1 subgroup (cofactor group point). 2. Submit as a KZG commitment in a data column sidecar via P2P. 3. Verify the deserialization function (bytes_to_kzg_commitment) REJECTS this input. 4. Similarly test bytes_to_kzg_proof with a cofactor point. 5. If the subgroup check is missing, construct a PoC where invalid data passes the pairing check due to the cofactor component. 6. Verify that the point-at-infinity (valid G1 point) is handled correctly.
executable_checks: Assert cofactor group point rejected by bytes_to_kzg_commitment
Assert cofactor group point rejected by bytes_to_kzg_proof
Assert valid G1 subgroup points accepted
Assert point-at-infinity handled correctly
Assert random 48-byte strings rejected (not on curve)
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-PCS-SECBND-PRECOND-003. Boundary property. Trust model edge EDGE-CELL-VERIFY-P2P: all inputs must be fully validated including subgroup checks. Missing subgroup checks break KZG proof soundness, allowing invalid data to be accepted as available by all nodes.

checklist_item.title: Falsify invariant: The CLZ opcode (0x1e) must produce deterministic results for all 256-bit inputs: for input 0 the result must be 256, for

reachability.notes: Non-deterministic CLZ results across clients cause consensus split on blocks containing CLZ.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the invariant at ACTION-COUNT-LEADING-ZEROS: The CLZ opcode (0x1e) must produce deterministic results for all 256-bit inputs: for input 0 the result must be 256, for any non-zero input the result must be the count of leading zero bits (range 0-255). 2. Design state transitions that could violate this invariant. 3. Test boundary conditions (min/max values, empty inputs, overflow). 4. Execute transitions and verify the invariant holds after each step. 5. If externally reachable, craft on-chain transactions or P2P messages to trigger violations. 6. Cross-client comparison to detect implementation divergence.
executable_checks: Construct state transition sequence targeting ACTION-COUNT-LEADING-ZEROS
Test with boundary values (0, max, overflow)
Verify invariant holds across all transitions
Cross-client comparison if consensus-relevant
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W0-EIP7939-INV-001. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Invariant. Primary: ACTION-COUNT-LEADING-ZEROS. Nodes: ['STATE-STACK-VALUE-READY', 'ACTION-POP-STACK-VALUE', 'ACTION-COUNT-LEADING-ZEROS', 'STATE-CLZ-COMPUTED', 'ACTION-PUSH-RESULT', 'STATE-RESULT-ON-STACK']. Edges: ['EDGE-INITIATE-CLZ', 'EDGE-VALUE-RETRIEVED', 'EDGE-COMPUTE-CLZ', 'EDGE-CLZ-DONE', 'EDGE-PUSH-CLZ', 'EDGE-RESULT-PUSHED'].

checklist_item.title: Verify postcondition: After each BPO fork activation, the blob parameters must be updated to the correct values for that BPO stage and all sub

reachability.notes: Wrong blob parameters after BPO transition would cause blocks to be incorrectly validated.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Execute the operation at ACTION-APPLY-BPO1-PARAMS. 2. Verify the postcondition: After each BPO fork activation, the blob parameters must be updated to the correct values for that BPO stage and all subsequent block validation must use the new parameters. 3. Check for unexpected side effects or state changes. 4. Test with edge-case inputs that stress the postcondition. 5. Cross-client comparison of output states.
executable_checks: Execute operation at ACTION-APPLY-BPO1-PARAMS with various inputs
Verify postcondition holds for all test cases
Check for unexpected side effects
Cross-client output comparison
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W1-EIP7607-POSTCOND-002. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Post-condition. Primary: ACTION-APPLY-BPO1-PARAMS. Nodes: ['ACTION-APPLY-BPO1-PARAMS', 'STATE-BPO1-ACTIVE', 'ACTION-APPLY-BPO2-PARAMS', 'STATE-BPO2-ACTIVE', 'ACTION-APPLY-BPO3-PARAMS', 'STATE-BPO3-ACTIVE']. Edges: ['EDGE-BPO1-APPLY-TO-ACTIVE', 'EDGE-BPO2-APPLY-TO-ACTIVE', 'EDGE-BPO3-APPLY-TO-ACTIVE'].

checklist_item.title: Falsify invariant: Throughout the receipt transfer process, the semantic content of receipts (transaction results, logs, gas usage) MUST re

reachability.notes: Encoding must preserve all semantic data

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the invariant at ACTION-ENCODE-RECEIPTS-ETH69: Throughout the receipt transfer process, the semantic content of receipts (transaction results, logs, gas usage) MUST remain unchanged. The eth/69 format change removes only the bloom filter from transmission; all other data MUST be preserved exactly. 2. Design state transitions that could violate this invariant. 3. Test boundary conditions (min/max values, empty inputs, overflow). 4. Execute transitions and verify the invariant holds after each step. 5. If externally reachable, craft on-chain transactions or P2P messages to trigger violations. 6. Cross-client comparison to detect implementation divergence.
executable_checks: Construct state transition sequence targeting ACTION-ENCODE-RECEIPTS-ETH69
Test with boundary values (0, max, overflow)
Verify invariant holds across all transitions
Cross-client comparison if consensus-relevant
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W2-EIP7642-RECEIPT-INV-01. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Invariant. Primary: ACTION-ENCODE-RECEIPTS-ETH69. Nodes: ['ACTION-ENCODE-RECEIPTS-ETH69', 'STATE-RECEIPTS-ENCODED', 'ACTION-DECODE-RECEIPTS']. Edges: ['EDGE-ENCODE', 'EDGE-ENCODED', 'EDGE-DECODE'].

checklist_item.title: Falsify invariant: The proposer_lookahead vector length must always equal (MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1) * SLOTS_PER_EPOCH after initialization a

reachability.notes: Wrong vector length would cause out-of-bounds access or missing proposer assignments.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the invariant at STATE-LOOKAHEAD-INITIALIZED: The proposer_lookahead vector length must always equal (MIN_SEED_LOOKAHEAD + 1) * SLOTS_PER_EPOCH after initialization and after each epoch boundary processing. 2. Design state transitions that could violate this invariant. 3. Test boundary conditions (min/max values, empty inputs, overflow). 4. Execute transitions and verify the invariant holds after each step. 5. If externally reachable, craft on-chain transactions or P2P messages to trigger violations. 6. Cross-client comparison to detect implementation divergence.
executable_checks: Construct state transition sequence targeting STATE-LOOKAHEAD-INITIALIZED
Test with boundary values (0, max, overflow)
Verify invariant holds across all transitions
Cross-client comparison if consensus-relevant
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W3-FULU-LOOKAHEAD-INV-001. Mindset: Formal Verification Engineer. Type: Invariant. Primary: STATE-LOOKAHEAD-INITIALIZED. Nodes: ['STATE-LOOKAHEAD-INITIALIZED', 'ACTION-COMPUTE-LOOKAHEAD-RANGE', 'ACTION-GET-PROPOSER-INDICES']. Edges: ['EDGE-COMPUTE-RANGE', 'EDGE-GET-INDICES', 'EDGE-POPULATED'].

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-RECEIVE-BLOB-TX: validate pre-condition at entry point

reachability.notes: Attacker can submit blob transactions with >6 blobs via P2P gossip.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Identify the trust boundary at EDGE-RECEIVE-BLOB-TX where untrusted data enters via P2P, Transaction. 2. Review input validation: Blob transactions received via P2P gossip MUST have blob count validated to be at most 6 before any further processing. 3. Craft malicious input that violates the stated precondition. 4. Send via P2P, Transaction and observe whether the node accepts, rejects, or crashes. 5. Compare behavior across at least 2 client implementations for consensus divergence.
executable_checks: Craft malformed input targeting EDGE-RECEIVE-BLOB-TX boundary
Send via P2P/transaction to a test node
Verify rejection with correct error code
Cross-client comparison of acceptance/rejection behavior
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W5-EIP7594-BLOBTX-PRECOND-001. Mindset: Boundary Guard. Property: Blob transactions received via P2P gossip MUST have blob count validated to be at most 6 before any further processing.. Graph: nodes=['STATE-BLOB-TX-RECEIVED', 'ACTION-VALIDATE-BLOB-COUNT', 'STATE-BLOB-COUNT-VALID', 'STATE-BLOB-COUNT-INVALID'], edges=['EDGE-RECEIVE-BLOB-TX', 'EDGE-CHECK-BLOB-COUNT', 'EDGE-BLOB-COUNT-OK', 'EDGE-BLOB-COUNT-FAIL'].

checklist_item.title: Verify Trust Boundary Integrity for EDGE-BLOCK-TO-SIDECAR-CELLS: validate invariant at entry point

reachability.notes: If EL sends old blob-level proofs instead of cell proofs, CL must detect and reject them.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. Review the boundary validation at EDGE-BLOCK-TO-SIDECAR-CELLS. 2. Verify that The BlobsBundle in Fulu MUST use cell-level KZG proofs exclusively. Blob-level KZG proofs from pre-Fulu formats MUST NOT be accepted or processed post-fork. 3. Test with edge-case inputs at the boundary. 4. Confirm consistent behavior across client implementations.
executable_checks: Test boundary validation at EDGE-BLOCK-TO-SIDECAR-CELLS
Verify correct acceptance/rejection behavior
Cross-client consistency check
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W6-FULU-VAL-SIDECAR-INV-001. Mindset: Boundary Guard. Property: The BlobsBundle in Fulu MUST use cell-level KZG proofs exclusively. Blob-level KZG proofs from pre-Fulu formats MUST NOT be accepted or processed post-fork.. Graph: nodes=['ACTION-CONSTRUCT-SIDECARS-FROM-CELLS'], edges=['EDGE-BLOCK-TO-SIDECAR-CELLS'].

checklist_item.title: Verify post-boundary handling at ACTION-BLOB-TO-POLYNOMIAL

reachability.notes: Malformed blob from Engine API could contain bytes that decode to values >= BLS_MODULUS, causing undefined behavior in field arithmetic.

checklist_item.detection_procedure: 1. After data crosses boundary at EDGE-BLOB-TO-POLY, verify internal handling at ACTION-BLOB-TO-POLYNOMIAL. 2. Check that validated data is correctly processed: Each field element in the polynomial MUST be less than BLS_MODULUS throughout the encoding pipeline (blob → polynomial → cells). 3. Verify no TOCTOU between boundary validation and internal use. 4. Confirm state transitions are correct post-validation.
executable_checks: Verify correct state transition at ACTION-BLOB-TO-POLYNOMIAL after boundary crossing
Check for TOCTOU vulnerabilities between validation and use
Confirm no data corruption in internal processing path
checklist_item.notes: Source: PROP-W7-PCS-ENCODE-INV-001. Supporting boundary check. Nodes under test: ['ACTION-BLOB-TO-POLYNOMIAL', 'ACTION-EVAL-TO-COEFF', 'STATE-POLYNOMIAL-COEFF-READY', 'ACTION-COMPUTE-CELLS-AND-PROOFS'].
